tldr: Cerbos (authorization policy engine) and Dfns (MPC wallet-as-a-service) are different categories, so this is a product pivot rather than a like-for-like migration.
why_switch: Your product is moving into crypto custody or wallet provisioning
You need MPC-backed key management with policy-driven approvals
You no longer require a general-purpose authorization engine
Compliance demands SOC 2 institutional-grade key custody
You want a managed wallet API rather than self-hosting authz
what_to_export: List of services calling the PDP
Mapping of Cerbos roles to business actions
export_method: Cerbos policies live as YAML in your repository — keep the directory under version control and snapshot decision logs via the Cerbos audit exporter (see Cerbos official docs).
import_method: Dfns does not consume authorization policies; instead, you model approval logic as Dfns policies attached to wallets and users. Recreate the relevant rules in Dfns per the Dfns official docs, focusing on signing approvals rather than general resource/action access.

concept_mapping.note: Confirm exact entity names in current docs
Authz is per-wallet rather than per-resource
Scope is narrower — only signing decisions

gotchas: Categories differ: Dfns will not replace generic authorization across your app
MPC key shares introduce custodial lock-in — plan exit strategy upfront
Dfns pricing is custom; budget a procurement cycle
Compliance review (KYC, AML, jurisdictions) will likely be required
Run wallet operations in a sandbox before moving real funds

email.subject: Cancellation: Cerbos Cloud / commercial plan
email.body:
Hi Cerbos team,

Our product direction has shifted toward wallet and key infrastructure (we are adopting Dfns), so we are winding down our Cerbos commercial usage. Please share the cancellation steps, the final invoice timing, and any data export we should run before account closure.

Thanks for the partnership.

Best regards
